title: Use the event_loop fixture
body:
Refs:
- https://github.com/simonw/datasette/issues/2461#issuecomment-2634920351

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://datasette--2463.org.readthedocs.build/en/2463/

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette end -->

files_modified.patch:
@@ -307,7 +307,7 @@ async def test_auth_with_dstok_token(ds_client, scenario, should_work):
 
 
 @pytest.mark.parametrize("expires", (None, 1000, -1000))
-def test_cli_create_token(app_client, expires):
+def test_cli_create_token(event_loop, app_client, expires):
     secret = app_client.ds._secret
     runner = CliRunner(mix_stderr=False)
     args = ["create-token", "--secret", secret, "test"]

steps.operation: Update the CLI token creation test to request pytest-asyncio's event loop fixture
steps.reason: Ensure the test runs with an explicit asyncio event loop available (matching pytest-asyncio expectations) when the CLI path touches async-aware application state via the app_client fixture.
steps.side_effects: Alters fixture resolution and test setup order for this test; may surface hidden event-loop scoping issues if other fixtures assume a different loop lifecycle.

steps.operation: Remove serial-only execution marker and require the event_loop fixture for the CLI token creation parametrized test
steps.reason: Replace reliance on serialized execution with an explicit event loop fixture to stabilize the test environment and avoid flakiness related to asyncio loop availability across parametrized runs.
steps.side_effects: Test will no longer be forced to run in isolation; can increase concurrency with other tests, which may expose shared-state coupling or race conditions in CLI/app initialization.

step_plan.summary: This PR updates CLI token creation tests to explicitly depend on pytest-asyncio's event_loop fixture, and removes a serial marker, improving reliability of tests that interact with async-aware application components.
